questions.question:
Given the programming constructs
I. assignment
II. for loops where the loop parameter cannot be changed within the loop
III. if-then-else
IV. forward go to
V. arbitrary go to
VI. non-recursive procedure call
VII. recursive procedure/function call
VIII. repeat loop,
which constructs will you not include in a programming language such that it should be possible to program the terminates (i.e., halting) function in the same programming language

(GATE CSE 1999)
questions.explanation: Click here for detail solution by gateoverflow

questions.question:
What is the result of the following program?

    program side-effect (input, output);
    var x, result: integer;
    function f (var x:integer:integer;
    begin
        x:x+1;f:=x;
    end
    begin
    x:=5;
    result:=f(x)*f(x);
    writeln(result);
    end


(GATE CSE 1998)
questions.explanation: Click here for detail solution by gateoverflow

questions.question:
What value would the following function return for the input x=95?

 Function fun (x:integer):integer;
Begin
    If x > 100 then fun = x- 10
    Else fun = fun(fun (x+11))
End;


(GATE CSE 1998)
questions.explanation: Click here for detail solution by gateoverflow

questions.question:
The conditional expansion facility of macro processor is provided to

(GATE CSE 1997)
options:
A.

test a condition during the execution of the expanded program

B.

to expand certain model statements depending upon the value of a condition during the execution of the expanded program

D.

to expand certain model statements depending upon the value of a condition during the process of macro expansion
questions.correct_answer:
D.

to expand certain model statements depending upon the value of a condition during the process of macro expansion
questions.explanation: Click here for detail solution by gateoverflow

questions.question:
Which of the following macros can put a macro assembler into an infinite loop?
I.

.MACRO M1, X
.IF EQ, X   ;if X=0 then
M1 X + 1
.ENDC
.IF NE, X   ;if X \(\neq\) O then
.WORD X  ;address (X) is stored here
.ENDC
.ENDM

II.
.MACRO M2, X
.IF EQ, X
M2 X
.ENDC
.IF NE, X
.WORD X + 1
.ENDC
.ENDM


(GATE CSE 1996)
questions.explanation: Click here for detail solution by gateoverflow

questions.question:
A language with string manipulation facilities uses the following operations

head(s): first character of a string
tail(s): all but exclude the first character of a string
 concat(s1, s2): s1s2
For the string "acbc" what will be the output of
concat(head(s), head(tail(tail(s))))


(GATE CSE 1995)

questions.question:
What is the value of X printed by the following program?

program COMPUTE (input, output);
var X:integer;
procedure FIND (X:real);
    begin
        X:=sqrt(X);
    end;
begin
    X:=2
    FIND(X);
    writeln(X);
end.


(GATE CSE 1995)

questions.question:
Assume that X and Y are non-zero positive integers. What does the following Pascal program segment do?

while X <> Y do
if  X > Y then
    X := X - Y
else
    Y := Y - X;
write(X);


(GATE CSE 1995)
options:
B.

Divides the larger number by the smaller number

X := -10; Y := 20;
If X > Y then if X < 0 then X := abs(X) else X := 2*X;

questions.question:
In which of the following cases is it possible to obtain different results for call-by-reference and call-by-name parameter passing methods?

(GATE CSE 1994)
options:
A.

Passing a constant value as a parameter

B.

Passing the address of an array as a parameter

C.

Passing an array element as a parameter
questions.correct_answer:
C.

Passing an array element as a parameter

questions.question:
An unrestricted use of the "goto" statement is harmful because

(GATE CSE 1994)
options:
A.

it makes it more difficult to verify programs

B.

it increases the running time of the programs

C.

it increases the memory required for the programs

D.

it results in the compiler generating longer machine code
questions.correct_answer:
A.

it makes it more difficult to verify programs

questions.question:
What does the following code do?

 var a, b: integer;
begin
    a:=a+b;
    b:=a-b;
    a:=a-b;
end;


(GATE CSE 1993)
questions.explanation: Click here for detail solution by gateoverflow

questions.question:
Refer to the PASCAL program shown below.

Program PARAM (input, output);
var m, n : integer;
procedure P (var, x, y : integer);
var m : integer;
begin
m : = 1;
x : = y + 1
end;
procedure Q (x:integer; vary : integer);
begin
x:=y+1;
end;
begin
m:=0; P(m,m); write (m);
n:=0; Q(n*1,n); write (n)
end

What is the scope of m declared in the main program?

(GATE CSE 1993)
questions.explanation: Click here for detail solution by gateoverflow

questions.question:
Refer to the PASCAL program shown below.

Program PARAM (input, output);
var m, n : integer;
procedure P (var, x, y : integer);
var m : integer;
begin
m : = 1;
x : = y + 1
end;
procedure Q (x:integer; vary : integer);
begin
x:=y+1;
end;
begin
m:=0; P(m,m); write (m);
n:=0; Q(n*1,n); write (n)
end

The value of n, output by the program PARAM is:

(GATE CSE 1993)
options:
A.

0, because n is the actual parameter corresponding to x in procedure Q.

B.

0, because n is the actual parameter to y in procedure Q.

C.

1, because n is the actual parameter corresponding to x in procedure Q.

D.

1, because n is the actual parameter corresponding to y in procedure Q.
questions.explanation: Click here for detail solution by gateoverflow
